REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1461 Revere Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sammie Dukes for (petitioner) Krystal Renee Smith-Jenkins. Charles Drexler (via court call) for the Jenkins Trust.

The Court stated that this is a sale for 50% interest in the estate, that the sale is for 100% interest including the trust's 50%, the court confirmed that Mr. Drexler is waives notice, that there is an issue regarding the authority to sell the trusts portion and that the court does not have anything establishing the trustee's right to go forward. Mr. Drexler stated that he has been the attorney for Marion & Gaynell Jenkins since 2011, that he is able to provide in detail that they wanted to execute this trust, that currently both of the Jenkins are incapacitated, that the oldest son Jackie Jenkins is named in the trust document as successor trustee, that Gaynell Jenkins is severely incapacitated and unable to care for her needs, that her son Alvin lives with her and provides 24 hour care, that she now needs to go to a facility and that proceeds from the sale will allow that, that he met with Marion Jenkins and Jackie Jenkins recently, that Marion is unable to manage finances or resist fraud, that 8.7 of the trust does not provide a mechanism for incapacity and that per 7.2 Jackie is designated as successor trustee. The Court confirmed with Mr. Drexler that there is nothing saying that they are incapacitated, stated that there is no evidence before it regarding any of this, that if it is a request of this court to make a determination to sell the property on behalf of the trust there should have been a proceeding to appoint Jackie as trustee or establish an appointment or evidence of the trust if it is necessary to make the determination. Mr. Drexler stated that they submitted a declaration of Jackie to serve as evidence but did not attach a copy of the trust, that they can submit a copy of the trust and that with the advent of HIPAA they no longer use the verification of physician regarding incapacitation as the successor trustees are unable to obtain them. The Court stated that trusts are legal technical instruments, that it may be necessary to have a legal determination made, that the court does have Jackie's declaration, confirmed that there is no one present today to overbid and that no one is aware of potential overbidders. The sale was confirmed for 50% interest in the estate at $365,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order) and subject to receiving a supplement/declaration with the trust and any other information regarding Jackie Jenkins necessary for intent to proceed. April 6, 2016 is the deadline for receiving additional information. An additional bond is not required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1461 Revere Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Continued To Mar-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sammie Dukes for Krystal Renee Smith-Jenkins.

Mr. Dukes stated that they are requesting a continuance, that the attorney for the trust will file a declaration and that an appraisal will be done tomorrow. The Court stated that there is also a question of notice with the correct hearing date being sent to the correct address of the party requesting special notice. Mr. Dukes stated that that is the attorney for the trust and that a declaration will be prepared. The report of sale and petition to confirm sale of real property was continued to March 30, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1461 Revere Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Continued To Mar-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sammie Dukes for Krystal Renee Smith-Jenkins.

The Court stated that there are procedural issues. Mr. Dukes reported that he responded with a filing yesterday but does not have a copy for the court. The Court asked if they have consent to sell 100% of the property and that the estate only has 50% interest. Mr. Dukes stated that they have a purchase agreement. The Court stated that it needs to know if the living trust has consented. Mr. Dukes stated that they have filed an agreement. The Court stated that it also needs a new reappraisal and asked if any over-bidders were expected and was told no. A short recess was taken to allow Mr. Dukes to obtain copies of filed documents to present to the court. The Court stated that the inventory & appraisal is dated October of 2012, that the only reappraisal on file is dated January 2015, that the reappraisal must be within 1 year of the sale and that no subsequent appraisal has been filed. Mr. Dukes stated that he has the correct reappraisal but that they filed a previous one in error. The Court stated that the buyer counter offer does not identify the trust at all, that it lists names but does not identify them as trustees of any trust and asked why no additional bond is required. Mr. Dukes stated that they only had a $10,000.00 bond because they were out of state and that he has no reason as to why an additional bond would not be necessary. The Court stated that they will have to file a reappraisal for sale, that they will need to file something to establish consent of the trust to sell its 1/2 interest, that they will have to amend or supplement the report of sale to reflect the bonding requirement or some reason why the additional bond is not required. The report of sale and petition to confirm sale of real property was continued to March 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
